facts.text: Valrico’s name means “rich valley” in Spanish, chosen by William G. Tousey in the 1880s to reflect the area’s fertile lands.
The Florida Central and Peninsular Railroad reached the area in 1890, sparking an influx of settlers and the town’s early growth.
In 1912, Lovett Brandon opened Valrico’s first general store, a landmark that still echoes the town’s early entrepreneurial spirit.
The town’s civic heart, the James McCabe Theater, began as the Valrico Civic Center in 1914 and was funded by a $3,500 donation from the Valrico Improvement Association.
Valrico’s mid‑1950s revival came when State Road 60 connected the town to Tampa’s Adamo Drive, placing it on a major Florida thoroughfare.
